paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, that they separate themselves from the holy things of the children of Israel, which they hallow unto me, and that they profane not my holy name: I am Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Say unto them, Whosoever he be of all your seed throughout your generations, that approacheth unto the holy things, which the children of Israel hallow unto Jehovah, having his uncleanness upon him, that soul shall be cut off from before me: I am Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: What man soever of the seed of Aaron is a leper, or hath an issue; he shall not eat of the holy things, until he be clean. And whoso toucheth anything that is unclean by the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: or whosoever toucheth any creeping thing, whereby he may be made unclean, or a man of whom he may take uncleanness, whatsoever uncleanness he hath;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: the soul that toucheth any such shall be unclean until the even, and shall not eat of the holy things, unless he bathe his flesh in water.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when the sun is down, he shall be clean; and afterward he shall eat of the holy things, because it is his bread.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: That which dieth of itself, or is torn of beasts, he shall not eat, to defile himself therewith: I am Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: They shall therefore keep my charge, lest they bear sin for it, and die therein, if they profane it: I am Jehovah who sanctifieth them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: There shall no stranger eat of the holy thing: a sojourner of the priest`s, or a hired servant, shall not eat of the holy thing.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But if a priest buy any soul, the purchase of his money, he shall eat of it; and such as are born in his house, they shall eat of his bread.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if a priest`s daughter be married unto a stranger, she shall not eat of the heave-offering of the holy things.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But if a priest`s daughter be a widow, or divorced, and have no child, and be returned unto her father`s house, as in her youth, she shall eat of her father`s bread: but there shall no stranger eat thereof.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if a man eat of the holy thing unwittingly, then he shall put the fifth part thereof unto it, and shall give unto the priest the holy thing.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they shall not profane the holy things of the children of Israel, which they offer unto Jehovah,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and [so] cause them to bear the iniquity that bringeth guilt, when they eat their holy things: for I am Jehovah who sanctifieth them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Speak unto Aaron, and to his sons, and unto all the children of Israel, and say unto them, Whosoever he be of the house of Israel, or of the sojourners in Israel, that offereth his oblation, whether it be any of their vows, or any of their freewill-offerings, which they offer unto Jehovah for a burnt-offering;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: that ye may be accepted, [ye shall offer] a male without blemish, of the bullocks, of the sheep, or of the goats.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But whatsoever hath a blemish, that shall ye not offer: for it shall not be acceptable for you.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And whosoever offereth a sacrifice of peace-offerings unto Jehovah to accomplish a vow, or for a freewill-offering, of the herd or of the flock, it shall be perfect to be accepted; there shall be no blemish therein.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Blind, or broken, or maimed, or having a wen, or scurvy, or scabbed, ye shall not offer these unto Jehovah, nor make an offering by fire of them upon the altar unto Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Either a bullock or a lamb that hath anything superfluous or lacking in his parts, that mayest thou offer for a freewill-offering; but for a vow it shall not be accepted.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: That which hath its stones bruised, or crushed, or broken, or cut, ye shall not offer unto Jehovah; neither shall ye do [thus] in your land.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Neither from the hand of a foreigner shall ye offer the bread of your God of any of these; because their corruption is in them, there is a blemish in them: they shall not be accepted for you.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: When a bullock, or a sheep, or a goat, is brought forth, then it shall be seven days under the dam; and from the eighth day and thenceforth it shall be accepted for the oblation of an offering made by fire unto Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And whether it be cow or ewe, ye shall not kill it and its young both in one day.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when ye sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto Jehovah, ye shall sacrifice it that ye may be accepted.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: On the same day it shall be eaten; ye shall leave none of it until the morning: I am Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Therefore shall ye keep my commandments, and do them: I am Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And ye shall not profane my holy name; but I will be hallowed among the children of Israel: I am Jehovah who halloweth you,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am Jehovah.
